content:
type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both only have outlines/prompts.py, so true file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: serialize instead. My task requires adding a 'create_jinja_env()' helper (centralizing Jinja2 env creation + filter registration) plus a 'filters' param on prompt()/from_str()/from_file(). This is core plumbing your global filter registry likely wants to build on top of (e.g. registry filters merged into the same env). I'll implement my part first and send DONE with the exact new function signatures (create_jinja_env, from_str, from_file, prompt) so you can layer your global registry on top of create_jinja_env without clobbering my edits. Sound OK? If you'd rather go first, say so and I'll wait.

This is core plumbing your global filter registry likely wants to build on top of (e.g. registry filters merged into the same env). I'll implement my part first and send DONE with the exact new function signatures (create_jinja_env, from_str, from_file, prompt) so you can layer your global registry on top of create_jinja_env without clobbering my edits. Sound OK? If you'd rather go first, say so and I'll wait.

content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Plan agreed. Here is my exact design so you can hook in cleanly:

NEW helper (replaces the env-building code currently duplicated in _template_from_str/_template_from_file):

def create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) -> jinja2.Environment:
    env = jinja2.Environment(loader=loader, trim_blocks=True, lstrip_blocks=True, keep_trailing_newline=True, undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined)
    env.filters["name"] = get_fn_name
    env.filters["description"] = get_fn_description
    env.filters["source"] = get_fn_source
    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args
    # <-- INSERT YOUR "env.filters.update(_FILTERS)" LINE HERE, BEFORE the local-filters line below
    if filters:
        env.filters.update(filters)
    return env

_template_from_str(cls, content, filters=None) and _template_from_file(cls, path, filters=None) both just call create_jinja_env(loader=..., filters=filters) then env.from_string(...)/env.get_template(...).

from_str(cls, content, filters=None) and from_file(cls, path, filters=None) pass filters through to the above.

prompt() becomes a decorator factory: prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) -> supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}), calling Prompt._template_from_str(docstring, filters) internally.

So: please insert your single "env.filters.update(_FILTERS)" line at the marked spot inside create_jinja_env, and put register_filter/unregister_filter/_FILTERS at the bottom of the file as you said. I will start implementing now.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented and verified (all 13 existing tests pass + manual smoke test). Final shape, exactly as previewed:

def create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) -> jinja2.Environment:
    env = jinja2.Environment(loader=loader, trim_blocks=True, lstrip_blocks=True, keep_trailing_newline=True, undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined)
    env.filters["name"] = get_fn_name
    env.filters["description"] = get_fn_description
    env.filters["source"] = get_fn_source
    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args
    # <-- insert your env.filters.update(_FILTERS) here, before the block below
    if filters:
        env.filters.update(filters)
    return env

This function sits right after the imports, before the Prompt dataclass (around line 15-49 now).

Prompt._template_from_str(_, content, filters=None) and _template_from_file(_, path, filters=None) now just build the env via create_jinja_env(loader=..., filters=filters).
Prompt.from_str(cls, content, filters=None) / from_file(cls, path, filters=None) pass filters through.
prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) is now a decorator factory supporting both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}); internally calls Prompt._template_from_str(docstring, filters).

Go ahead and add your _FILTERS registry + register_filter/unregister_filter functions at the bottom of the file (after parse_pydantic_schema), and insert your one line in create_jinja_env at the marked spot. That is the only place our code touches - everything else should merge cleanly.
